name: Chinese: Mongolian Beef 2
recipeInstructions:
Servings: 4
Preparation: Cut the tops of green onions into 2-inch pieces. Combine the sauce ingredients in a small bowl and stir thoroughly. Cut the steak across the grain into thin slices, about ½-inch deep by 2 inches long. In a large enough bowl to hold the meat, combine egg whites, salt, and water chestnut flour. Beat with a chopstick until frothy. Add steak and use fingers to coat each slice.
Deep-frying:
- In a wok, heat oil to moderately hot. When ready, a piece of coated meat will rise to the surface immediately.
- Fry the meat in small batches; drop one slice at a time to avoid sticking. Cook until lightly brown, about 1 minute. Drain on a Chinese strainer or paper bag.
Stir-frying:
- Remove all but 2 tablespoons of oil from the wok.
- With the wok at medium heat, quickly stir-fry green onions and ginger for about 20 seconds.
- Add sauce; bring to boil on high heat while stirring.
- Add beef all at once, and toss with sauce until beef is hot and coated.
- Push beef out of the sauce, dribble in cornstarch paste to lightly thicken. Recombine.
- Serve immediately.
